chapter.label: Tale 202
chapter.title: The life of the servant of GOD

paragraphs.text: ABIBAS, THE SON OF A WORLDLY MAN The father then died. <Abibas> gave to each brother his share of the inheritance and he took his own share too, but he gave it all to One of the [athers said there was a man living in the world who had the poor, leaving nothing for himself. He built a small cell into a pious son, pure and temperate in all things, who, from his which he could withdraw from the world and when the cell was childhood, had not drunk wine. It was his intention to withdraw completed, he fell ill. His ead was approaching. His <monastic> from the world. The father wanted him to become involved in brother was sitting with him, to whom the dying man said: ‘Go and business matters but the son was reluctant. There were other keep company with your household, for it is a holy day’ (it was the brothers. but he was the oldest. As his father's wishes and his own feast of the Holy Apostles). The brother replied: ‘How could I go could not be reconciled, the father was always reproaching him and and leave you? The other replied: ‘Go; and when the time comes, casting his temperance in his teeth, saying: ‘Why arc you not like I shall call you'. When the time came, he stood at the window and your brothers, and why do you not get yourself’ involved in business knocked. The brother heard and obeyed the sick man's signal to affairs?’ The son endured it all in silence; everybody loved him for come. As soon as he entered, the older brother surrendered his soul his piety and his moderation <sGphrosiné>. to the Lord. Everybody was amazed and glorified God, saying: ‘His end was worthy of the Jove with which he loved Christ’.
